title: Mangottu Bhagavathi Temple
tagline: Mangottu Bhagavathi Temple — spiritual town of Kerala

overview.short: Mangottu Bhagavathi Temple is in Athipotta, 24 km from Palakkad via Alathur.
overview.description: Mangottu Bhagavathi Temple is in Athipotta, 24 km from Palakkad via Alathur. The Hindu goddess of the temple, Mangottu Bhagavathi, is considered the younger sister of Parakkattu Bhagwathi who has a temple in Kavasseri near by.

routes.byAir: Fly to nearest airport + road transfer

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Chulanur
topPlaces.description: Chulanur is a small village in Palakkad district of Kerala state, south India. Chulanur is famous for peacocks.

topPlaces.name: Tarur-I
topPlaces.description: Tarur-I is a village in Palakkad district in the state of Kerala, India.

topPlaces.name: Athipotta
topPlaces.description: Athipotta is a small village near Alathur in the Palakkad district of Kerala state, south India. It's about 30 km from Palakkad town and 10 km from Alathur.

topPlaces.name: Choolannur Pea Fowl Sanctuary
topPlaces.description: Choolannur Pea Fowl Sanctuary is a bird sanctuary located in Chulanur village in Palakkad district of Kerala state, south India. The place where sanctuary is located is locally known as Mayiladumpara, which literally means "the rock where peacocks dance".

topPlaces.name: Vadakkethara
topPlaces.description: Vadakkethara is a village in Thrissur district in the state of Kerala, India.

topPlaces.name: Padur
topPlaces.description: Padur (or Padoor) (Malayam: പാടൂര്) is a village in Palakkad district, Kerala, India. The village is prominently known for its temple of Sree Panickanar, where a yearly festival called Padur Vela is celebrated and is well attended for Vela-Poorams in the Palakkad district.

topPlaces.name: Manappadam
topPlaces.description: Manappadam is a village in the Palakkad district of Kerala state, south India. It is in Puthucode Panchayath.

topPlaces.name: Pazhayannur
topPlaces.description: Pazhayannur is a town and panchayath in Thrissur district, in the state of Kerala, India. The eminent mridangist, Palghat Mani Iyer was born here.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Mangottu Bhagavathi Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Mangottu Bhagavathi Temple is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Mangottu Bhagavathi Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Cochin International Airport, Kochi (60 km) and the nearest railway station is Coimbatore Junction (63 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Mangottu Bhagavathi Temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Mangottu Bhagavathi Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Mangottu Bhagavathi Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Mangottu Bhagavathi Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Mangottu Bhagavathi Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Chulanur, Tarur-I, Athipotta, Choolannur Pea Fowl Sanctuary, Vadakkethara.
